name: Gastroenteritis
notes: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, often related to diet changes, infections, or toxins.
recommendedActions: monitor hydration
withhold food briefly as directed by a veterinarian
contact veterinarian if vomiting or diarrhea persists beyond 24 hours
seek emergency care if vomiting is severe or contains blood

name: Pancreatitis
notes: Inflammation of the pancreas that can be acute or chronic and is often underdiagnosed in cats.
recommendedActions: schedule prompt veterinary exam
supportive care for pain, nausea, and hydration
monitor for worsening vomiting or lethargy
follow vet instructions on diet modification

name: Hepatic Lipidosis
notes: Fat accumulation in the liver, often triggered by a period of anorexia; common life-threatening liver disease in cats.
recommendedActions: seek emergency veterinary care
aggressive nutritional support may be required
monitor liver values as directed by veterinarian

name: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
notes: Chronic inflammation of the intestines that often causes waxing and waning GI signs.
recommendedActions: schedule non-emergency veterinary visit
discuss diet trials and long-term management
monitor body weight and appetite trends

name: Feline Upper Respiratory Infection
notes: Common complex of viral and bacterial infections affecting the upper airways, especially in young or shelter cats.
recommendedActions: monitor eating and drinking
keep nose and eyes clean of discharge
contact veterinarian if breathing becomes labored or appetite is poor
isolate from other cats if contagious

name: Feline Asthma
notes: Chronic inflammatory disease of the lower airways, often triggered by allergens or irritants.
recommendedActions: seek veterinary evaluation for coughing or breathing episodes
minimize exposure to smoke, dust, and aerosols
use inhaled or oral medications as prescribed
treat acute respiratory distress as an emergency

name: Feline Infectious Peritonitis
notes: Serious viral disease associated with feline coronavirus mutation; can affect multiple organs.
recommendedActions: contact veterinarian promptly if FIP is suspected
diagnostic testing and supportive care as directed
discuss evolving treatment options with veterinarian

name: Feline Calicivirus Infection
notes: Viral infection that often accompanies upper respiratory disease and can cause mouth ulcers and lameness.
recommendedActions: monitor appetite and hydration
offer soft or warmed food if mouth is sore
consult veterinarian for pain control and supportive care
keep cat isolated from other cats when actively sick

name: Feline Lower Urinary Tract Disease
notes: A group of conditions affecting the bladder and urethra; can be painful and may lead to obstruction, especially in male cats.
recommendedActions: treat any straining or blood in urine as urgent
contact veterinarian the same day
seek emergency care if the cat cannot pass urine
increase water intake as advised by veterinarian

name: Urinary Tract Infection
notes: Bacterial infection of the bladder or urinary tract, more common in older cats or those with underlying disease.
recommendedActions: schedule veterinary visit for urinalysis and culture
follow prescribed antibiotic course if indicated
monitor for recurrence of signs

name: Urethral Obstruction
symptoms: straining with little or no urine
notes: Life-threatening blockage of the urethra, most common in male cats; requires immediate veterinary treatment.
recommendedActions: seek emergency veterinary care immediately
do not wait more than a few hours if the cat cannot urinate
monitor for recurrence after treatment

name: Chronic Kidney Disease
notes: Common chronic disease in older cats where kidney function gradually declines over time.
recommendedActions: schedule veterinary exam and bloodwork
discuss kidney-supportive diet and medications
monitor body weight, appetite, and hydration at home

name: Hyperthyroidism
notes: Very common endocrine disorder in older cats caused by overactive thyroid glands.
recommendedActions: schedule bloodwork to confirm diagnosis
discuss medication, diet, or radioactive iodine therapy with veterinarian
monitor weight and appetite regularly

name: Diabetes Mellitus
notes: Metabolic disease in which the body cannot properly regulate blood sugar.
recommendedActions: schedule veterinary visit for blood and urine testing
start insulin and diet management as prescribed
monitor for signs of low blood sugar and other complications

name: Obesity
notes: Excess body weight that increases risk for diabetes, arthritis, and other diseases.
recommendedActions: work with veterinarian on a weight-loss plan
adjust calorie intake and treat frequency
increase controlled play and activity

name: Periodontal Disease
notes: Common dental disease involving plaque, tartar, and gum inflammation that can progress to tooth loss.
recommendedActions: schedule dental examination
professional dental cleaning as recommended
implement home dental care if possible

name: Osteoarthritis
notes: Degenerative joint disease that causes chronic pain and reduced mobility, often under-recognized in older cats.
recommendedActions: discuss pain management and joint-support therapies with veterinarian
provide soft bedding and easy access to resources
monitor activity level and comfort over time
